title: Water ejecting apparatus
abstract: Provided is a water ejecting apparatus including a case and a water ejection unit coupled to one side of the case, wherein the water ejection unit includes a first lifting cover connected to the case and allowing a lifting gear to be fixed thereto, a second lifting cover movably accommodated inside the first lifting cover, a lifting motor coupled to the second lifting cover and configured to interwork with the lifting motor, and a water ejection nozzle installed at a lower end of the second lifting cover and configured to allow water to be ejected therethrough, wherein the lifting gear has gear teeth provided on one side thereof in an up-down direction and at least one reinforcing hole or reinforcing recess provided on the other side thereof.
claims: Claims ( 20 ) A water ejecting apparatus including a case and a water ejection unit coupled to one side of the case and at least partially protruding therefrom, wherein the water ejection unit comprises: a first lifting cover connected to the case and allowing a lifting gear to be fixed thereto; a second lifting cover movably accommodated inside the first lifting cover; a lifting motor coupled to the second lifting cover and configured to interwork with the lifting motor; and a water ejection nozzle installed at a lower end of the second lifting cover and configured to allow water to be ejected therethrough, wherein the lifting gear has gear teeth provided on one side thereof in an up-down direction and at least one reinforcing hole or reinforcing recess provided on the other side thereof. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first lifting gear comprises a plate where the lifting gear is provided. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 2, wherein the reinforcing hole is provided on a front surface of the plate or the reinforcing recess is formed to be recessed backward. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 3, wherein the reinforcing hole or the reinforcing recess is provided in plurality and the plurality of reinforcing holes or the plurality of reinforcing recesses are spaced apart from each other in the up-down direction. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 3, wherein the reinforcing holes or the reinforcing recesses are arranged at the same interval as an interval between the gear teeth of the lifting gear. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 3, wherein the center of the reinforcing hole or the reinforcing recess is disposed at a position horizontal to a highest gear ridge of the lifting gear The water ejecting apparatus of claim 1, wherein a flat reinforcing plate thinner than the gear teeth is provided on one side of the lifting gear. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 1, wherein the gear module comprises: a gear bracket coupled to the second lifting cover; and a gear rotatably installed at the gear bracket and engaged with the lifting gear, wherein the gear rotates along the lifting gear according to an operation of the lifting motor, and the second lifting cover relatively moves in the up-down direction with respect to the first lifting cover. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first lifting cover further comprises a guide rail spaced apart from the lifting gear, extending in the up-down direction, and provided in a plurality of seating recesses spaced apart from each other in the up-down direction, and wherein the gear bracket comprises a guide rail protrusion disposed to be in close contact with the guide rail and inserted into the plurality of seating recesses according to a vertical movement. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 9, wherein the first lifting cover comprises a water ejection opening provided between the lifting gear and the guide rail, and a water ejection pipe extends from an inside of the case and connected to the water ejection nozzle through the water ejection opening. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 9, wherein the plurality of seating recesses comprise a first seating recess and second seating recess provided below the first seating recess, and wherein the second lifting cover moves in the up-down direction so as to be lifted until the guide rail protrusion is inserted into the first seating recess and lowered until the guide rail protrusion is inserted into the second seating recess. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 1, wherein the lifting motor comprises a motor shaft and a motor gear connected to the motor shaft, and wherein the gear module comprises: a first gear engaged with the motor gear; a second gear coaxially provided with the first gear; a third gear engaged with the second gear; and a fourth gear coaxially provided with the third gear and engaged with the lifting gear. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 12, wherein rotating shafts of the first gear, the second gear, the third gear, and the fourth gear are located above the motor shaft of the motor. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 12, wherein the lifting gear is fixed to one side of the first lifting cover, and wherein rotating shafts of the first gear, the second gear, the third gear, and the fourth gear are located to stagger to one side with respect to the motor shaft of the motor. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 12, wherein rotating shafts of the third gear and the fourth gear are arranged to stagger to one side with respect to rotating shafts of the first gear and the second gear. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 12, wherein rotating shafts of the third gear and the fourth gear are arranged above rotating shafts of the first gear and the second gear. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 12, wherein the first and second lifting covers are convex forward, and wherein the lifting motor is disposed at a front in the second lifting cover and the gears are arranged at a rear with respect to the lifting motor. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 1, wherein the water ejection unit further comprises a water ejection top cover extending from a top cover forming an upper surface of the case and configured to cover the first lifting cover. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 18, wherein the water ejection top cover comprises an input unit to which a predetermined command is input. The water ejecting apparatus of claim 19, wherein the input unit comprises a lifting input unit, and wherein the water ejecting apparatus further comprises a controller configured to operate the lifting motor according to an input of the lifting input unit to lift the water ejection nozzle.
